title: Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaura Dasa I SB 3.6.17 I 11.11.2021
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 6, Text No. 17
Translation in purport by His Divine Grace
When the ears of the gigantic form became manifested,
all the controlling deities of the directions
entered into them with the hearing principles
by which all the living entities hear and take advantage of sound.
The ear is the most important instrument in the body of the living entity.
Sound is the most important medium for carrying the message
The perfection of all sound or knowledge
enters through the ear and makes one's life perfect.
The entire Vedic system of knowledge
is received by aural reception only
and thus sound is the most important source of knowledge.
So, how the living entities can experience
If one wants to achieve perfection,
One has to hear perfect sound.
the perfect knowledge should be heard.
So, for that purpose Karanavasya Vinirbhinao
The ears became manifested in the gigantic form
The controlling deities of the directions
Along with the Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya, the controlling deities of the directions, they entered into this new department along with the Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya.
Every department comes with its own principles,
So, by which Siddhyam Yena Prapadhyate
if one doesn't follow the Vedic knowledge.
If somebody doesn't know the Vedic knowledge,
if somebody doesn't have the knowledge of the Vedic knowledge,
if somebody doesn't know the Vedic knowledge,
doesn't take advantage of the sound which is depicted in the Shastras. When
someone reads the Shastras or recites the Shastras, the sound which comes, that
has to be heard. So there are some principles. So if somebody follows such
principles and then hears that sound, then Siddhim, there is a perfection of
life. Generally we will not be able to hear properly. Our attention will be
diverted if mind is polluted. And mind remains polluted if the living entities
are engaged in the four sinful activities.
So that's why Acharya's instructions, they have to be followed. And anyone who
follows such instructions of practicing these principles, then he will become
pure in mind and he will be able to give full attention to hearing.
So Prabhupada is saying here that ears are the most important instruments in the body.
It is said that when a person is dying, various parts start failing in his body.
Various senses start failing. But the ears, they remain till the last moment.
They are the last ones to fail. So when somebody is about to die also, you can just say Hare Krishna. Last moment you can hear Hare Krishna and then die.

So this is the most important instrument in the body. Many times we think ears, eyes are the most important. Some other sense is the most important. But actually ears are the most important instruments in the body.
And why is that? It can hear sound.
Ear is a knowledge acquiring sense.
Ear is a knowledge acquiring sense.
So through sound, it can acquire knowledge. The ears can acquire knowledge.
Now some sound has come. Now you know somebody has entered the temple hall.
Now some bells are ringing. We know. Dities are offered, Naivedya. See? Knowledge.
So sound is such a powerful instrument, powerful media for gaining knowledge.
If you can hear perfect sound, perfect knowledge, then one's life becomes perfect.
So if someone doesn't follow the Shastra, then na siddhim, na sukham.
There is no Siddhi, there is no happiness.
So now why one doesn't follow the Shastra? Because kamakarata, driven by lust.
So when we are driven by lust, we break all Shastric principles.
So that's why it is mentioned here that the perfection comes through the ears.
When one listens to the perfect message.
One of the hearing principles is that you should hear the truth.
That is depicted in the Vedic knowledge.
One should be eager to hear the truth.
One should not hear such kind of sounds, noise.
I mean if it happens to be there, then you are forced to hear, that's okay.
But then one is not eager to listen to all these things.
Eagerness is how can I receive perfect sound?
And the perfect sound comes from the lotus lips of the Acharya.
The sound that comes from the mouth of an Acharya is the perfect sound.
So anybody who is eager to listen to Acharya, he wants to hear from the spiritual master.
More and more he is properly situated.
He is on the path of perfectional life.
That how he was very eager to listen to his spiritual master.
And then when there is an opportunity for even going for some pilgrimage.
Prabhupada chose to remain in the class.
He wanted to attend the class of his spiritual master.
Prabhupada says that has actually pleased his master.
His master Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati was very pleased with him.
Because Prabhupada was very eager to listen to him.
He is listening to him, although he could not understand.
Bhakti Siddhanta was explaining many things.
He, Prabhupada says that he could not understand.
But still he simply sat there and listened to them.
He simply sat there and listened to the sound.
The potency of that sound is there.
And that sound, if it penetrates through the ears.
So this perfection through sound is called Mantra Siddhi.
Anybody who listens to the Mantra.
Then there is a Mantra Siddhi.
Such a simple system it is.
All that he has to do is simply listen to this sound attentively.
Particularly the words of the Acharya.
So Acharya's words have to be taken submissively.
And one more is called the Rithvik camp.
Actually it is a loose word.
Prabhupada has not created any pure devotee.
That Prabhupada is not capable of creating any pure devotees.
They have been serving Prabhupada's movement.
For him to become an Acharya.
They are not qualified to sit in the Acharya Peetha.
If Prabhupada can become a Guru.
Then Rupa Goswami can also become a Guru.
I will give you Rupa Goswami Diksha.
Actually these are not the arguments.
These are all called straw arguments.
An attempt to divert the attention.
You can go to Rupa Goswami.
Or you can go to Manu.
This is not at all an argument.
One has to understand what is the Acharya's order, what is the Acharya's wish.
Don't bring quotes, stories, some Mahabharata, this Bharata, that Bharata.
And then try to prove your point.
Understand what is that Acharya wants.
What is his final direction on this subject matter?
Once you ascertain what is his final direction, then you bring all kinds of quotes, letters, conversations, everything to support that.
First is Guru's order, Acharya's wish.
And then if you see the 9th July letter, it is not actually a letter.
It is a directive that is given to the entire society.
It is addressed to the entire movement.
It is addressed to the temple presidents and the GBCs.
It is addressed to the top management of the movement.
It is not directed towards a single individual devotee.
It is addressed to the official positions within the organization.
One should not think that this is written to some individual devotee and then there something is
written so that becomes the basis for ever-perfection of life.
So, that 20th May came and those things are discussed, Reethvik word came out, disciple
of Guru, disciple word came out.
There were so many things came out in the conversation.
Yea there are many things came out.

After that also there were few meetings. There also many things came out. Ultimately, a final direction has come to the entire movement in writing, signed by none other than His Divine Grace. And that is July 9th, 1977 direction.

What does that say? That document say? Did the document appoint these 11 as Diksha Gurus or as Ritwiks? It's very clear. Anybody can access this direction, July 9th. And then see what does Prabhupada say?
Prabhupada is clearly mentioning that these 11 people are only Ritwiks.
July 9th, 1978 B.T.G. Saswaroop Kuswami declares that Prabhupada has appointed these 11 as successors to Shri Prabhupada. Now that is surprising. Where did you get that direction?
So Ritwiks are also saying 11. Guru Camp is also saying 11. But Ritwiks have got something in writing. There is a written direction.
Which says that these 11 are only Ritwiks and the newly initiated disciples are disciples of His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada.
So clearly Prabhupada mentions. In fact, three times he refers to He remaining as a current link.
He says after the initiation is done, those names should be included in the Prabhupada's disciples book.
See, and then in the beginning when he is saying Ritwik, he says representative of the Acharya, that means He remains the Acharya, He remains the current link.
And that system is running for the four months, it is running. July, August, September, October, November, till November 14, system is running.
Now where is the direction for stopping this?
Stopping something which is going on, something which is already in practice going on.
Now what is the direction, additional direction in writing that you got to change such a system?
Prabhupada is overseeing such implementation for the last three, four months.
The whole world is following that. Now what is that which has triggered you to change the system?
So what is Guru's order has to be clearly ascertained. Otherwise, Siddhim Ena Prapadhyate, one will not experience Siddhi perfection.
Now you cannot try to bring your own arguments and say, no, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present.
No, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present.
No, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present.
No, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present.
In order to give Deeksha, Guru has to be physically present.
Now where are the proofs from the Shastra to prove these points?
Now where are the proofs from the Shastra to prove these points?
And for us, the Shastra is Prabhupada's books.
And for us, the Shastra is Prabhupada's books.
Prabhupada's books and whatever Prabhupada has given, from his conversations, from his directions,
All that has to be used to prove.
All that has to be used to prove.
